entries.title: How Snowflake Built a $100M Pipeline with Account-Based Marketing
entries.snippet: Snowflake's account-based marketing program targeted 500 enterprise accounts with personalized multi-channel campaigns, generating over $100M in qualified pipeline within 18 months.

entries.title: How HubSpot Grew to $1B ARR with Inbound Marketing
entries.snippet: HubSpot pioneered inbound marketing as both a strategy and product, growing from startup to $1B ARR by giving away free tools and educational content that attracted millions of marketers.

entries.title: Slack's Product-Led Growth Strategy: From 0 to $27B Acquisition
entries.snippet: Slack grew by letting individual users adopt the product freely within organizations, then converting organic usage into enterprise contracts — a bottom-up GTM strategy that led to Salesforce's $27.7B acquisition.

entries.title: How Gong Built a Category with Data-Driven Content Marketing
entries.snippet: Gong used its own revenue intelligence data to publish original research that competitors couldn't replicate, turning proprietary data into a content marketing moat that drove category leadership.

entries.title: How Notion Cracked Enterprise Sales with a Product-Led Flywheel
entries.snippet: Notion grew to $10B by letting individual users pull the product into enterprises, then layering a sales team on top only after organic adoption hit critical mass within target accounts.

entries.title: Adobe's ABM Transformation: From Mass Marketing to Precision Targeting
entries.snippet: Adobe restructured its entire B2B marketing organization around ABM, moving from broad demand gen to account-level orchestration and seeing a 3x increase in engagement rates with target accounts.

entries.title: How Atlassian Scaled to $3B Revenue with Zero Traditional Sales
entries.snippet: Atlassian built a $3B+ business with no outbound sales team, relying on free tiers, self-serve pricing, and community-driven adoption to land and expand within enterprises globally.

entries.title: The Rippling GTM Playbook: How Parker Conrad Built a $13B Compound Startup
entries.snippet: Rippling's compound startup strategy bundles HR, IT, and finance products to increase deal sizes and reduce churn, with a multi-product GTM motion that creates natural cross-sell within accounts.

entries.title: How Salesforce Created the SaaS Marketing Playbook
entries.snippet: Salesforce pioneered the modern SaaS go-to-market playbook: free trials, customer success teams, annual conferences, and ecosystem-driven growth that defined how B2B software is sold today.

entries.title: How ZoomInfo Built a Data-Driven Demand Generation Engine
entries.snippet: ZoomInfo used its own intent data platform to power its demand gen engine, targeting accounts showing buying signals and achieving 40% higher conversion rates than traditional lead scoring.
